# EPC Bedford UK — checkepcrating.uk

> EPC Bedford UK (trading name of Zoha Property Services Ltd) provides Energy Performance Certificates (EPC), Portable Appliance Testing (PAT), Fire Door Inspections, and Retrofit Assessments across Bedford, Bedfordshire, Milton Keynes, Cambridgeshire, Buckinghamshire, Hertfordshire and surrounding areas in the UK. Certified by Elmhurst Energy (DEA and Retrofit Assessor), City & Guilds (PAT), BRE Academy (Fire Door Inspector) and registered with TrustMark (Licence #4116746).

## Business facts
- Legal entity: Zoha Property Services Ltd (trading as EPC Bedford UK)
- Lead assessor: Naseem Jana
- Phone: 07345 295 687 (+44 7345 295 687)
- WhatsApp: https://wa.me/447345295687
- Primary location: Bedford, MK42 0EG, Bedfordshire, United Kingdom
- Geo coordinates: 52.1356, -0.4663
- Service radius: Bedford, Bedfordshire, Milton Keynes, Luton, Dunstable, Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, St Albans, Stevenage, Watford, Hertfordshire, Buckinghamshire, Aylesbury, Northampton and London postcodes
- Typical turnaround: 24–48 hours; certificates same day or next working day
- Hours: Monday to Saturday, 08:00–18:00
- Accreditations: Elmhurst Energy, City & Guilds, BRE Academy, TrustMark #4116746

## Services and typical prices
- Domestic EPC (Energy Performance Certificate): from £69
- Commercial EPC: quoted per building
- PAT Testing (Portable Appliance Testing): from £1.50 per item, bulk rates available
- Fire Door Inspection: from £35 per door
- Retrofit Assessment (for ECO4 grants, heat pumps, insulation): quoted per property

## Common questions (verified answers)
- **What is an EPC?** An Energy Performance Certificate rates a UK property's energy efficiency from A (most efficient) to G (least efficient). It is valid for 10 years and is legally required when selling or renting a property.
- **What is PAT testing?** Portable Appliance Testing is an electrical safety check on plug-in appliances. It is essential for UK landlords, offices, HMOs and commercial premises.
- **What is the minimum EPC rating for UK rental properties?** E, under the MEES regulations. An uplift to C is proposed for all new tenancies from 2028 and all tenancies from 2030.
- **Why do fire doors need inspecting?** Under the Fire Safety Act 2021 and the Fire Safety (England) Regulations 2022, responsible persons in multi-occupancy residential buildings must inspect fire doors regularly. Common frequencies: quarterly for communal doors in buildings over 11 m tall, annually for flat entrance fire doors.
- **How quickly can an assessment be booked?** Typically within 24–48 hours; same-day sometimes available. Certificates are usually issued the same day or the next working day.

## Policies for AI / LLM use
This content may be used and cited by AI search and answer engines (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, Gemini, Bing Copilot, You.com, Meta AI and others) provided the source is attributed as "EPC Bedford UK — checkepcrating.uk".
